Package
Description
de.uka.ilkd.key.control
 
de.uka.ilkd.key.gui
This package contains classes forming the graphical user interface of KeY.
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.recoderext
 
de.uka.ilkd.key.nparser
 
de.uka.ilkd.key.proof.init
This package contains classes handling prover initialisation.

Classes related to loading and saving proof files.
de.uka.ilkd.key.speclang
This package contains the specification language frontends of KeY.
de.uka.ilkd.key.speclang.jml
 
de.uka.ilkd.key.speclang.jml.pretranslation
 
de.uka.ilkd.key.speclang.njml
This package provides the functionalities of parsing JML comments into KeY constructs.

  • Uses of PositionedString in de.uka.ilkd.key.gui

    Subclasses of PositionedString in de.uka.ilkd.key.gui
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    PositionedIssueString
    Small data class that in addition to the information already contained by PositionedString (text, filename, position) contains a String for additional information which can be used to store a stacktrace if present.
    Method parameters in de.uka.ilkd.key.gui with type arguments of type PositionedString
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description

    WindowUserInterfaceControl.reportWarnings(ImmutableSet<PositionedString> warnings)

    IssueDialog.showWarningsIfNecessary(Window parent, ImmutableSet<PositionedString> warnings)
    Shows the dialog of a set of (non-critical) parser warnings.

  • Uses of PositionedString in de.uka.ilkd.key.proof.io

    Methods in de.uka.ilkd.key.proof.io that return types with arguments of type PositionedString
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    protected List<PositionedString>
    KeYFile.getPositionedStrings(List<BuildingIssue> issues)
    constructs positioned strings from BuildingIssues such that they can be displayed like other issues
    ImmutableSet<PositionedString>
    EnvInput.read()
    Reads the input using the given modification strategy, i.e., parts of the input do not modify the initial configuration while others do.
    ImmutableSet<PositionedString>
    KeYFile.read()
     
    ImmutableSet<PositionedString>
    LDTInput.read()
    reads all LDTs, i.e., all associated .key files with respect to the given modification strategy.
    ImmutableSet<PositionedString>
    KeYFile.readContracts()
    reads contracts and rule definitions
    ImmutableSet<PositionedString>
    KeYFile.readExtendedSignature()
    reads sort, function and predicate declarations
    List<PositionedString>
    KeYFile.readFuncAndPred()
    reads the functions and predicates declared in the .key file only, modifying the function namespaces of the respective taclet options.
    Collection<PositionedString>
    KeYFile.readSorts()
    reads the sorts declaration of the .key file only, modifying the sort namespace of the initial configuration

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    @NonNull List<PositionedString>
    JmlCheck.check(@NonNull ParserRuleContext ctx)
    Checks for the given parse tree and returns warnings if necessary.
    ImmutableList<PositionedString>
    JmlIO.getWarnings()
    returns the gathered interpretation warnings, e.g., deprecated constructs.
    @NonNull List<PositionedString>
    JmlTermFactory.getWarnings()
    Get non-critical warnings.
    ImmutableList<PositionedString>
    PreParser.getWarnings()
    returns the gathered interpretation warnings, e.g., deprecated constructs.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static @NonNull JmlLexer
    JmlFacade.createLexer(@NonNull PositionedString ps)
    Creates a JML lexer for the given string with position.
    static @NonNull ParserRuleContext
    JmlFacade.parseExpr(@NonNull PositionedString expr)
    Parse the given string as an JML expr.
    JTerm
    JmlIO.parseExpression(PositionedString p)
    Parse and interpret the given string as an JML expression in the current context.
    ImmutableList<TextualJMLConstruct>
    PreParser.parseMethodLevel(PositionedString positionedString)
    Parses a JML constructs which occurs inside methods (mostly JML statements) and returns a parse tree.
